cfgsync.apply.confirm: This will write the repository's config files to their locations on this machine, overwriting same-named files (may affect existing config). Continue?
cfgsync.installed.none: No catalog software detected as installed on this machine.
cfgsync.installed.subtitle: Scan which of the catalog's apps are installed on this machine and save them as a reusable profile; on a new device, pick that profile in the Install Center to replicate them in one click.
cfgsync.installed.title: Sync installed software
cfgsync.ssh.confirm: This will generate a new ed25519 SSH key on this machine and apply the ssh config (existing files are backed up first). Continue?
cfgsync.ssh.confirmRegister: This will generate a new ed25519 SSH key, apply the ssh config, and register the public key on your GitHub account. Continue?
cfgsync.env.confirm: This writes the environment configs captured in the repository (SSH / GPG / git credentials / Codex / Claude …) back onto this machine, overwriting same-named files (backed up automatically). Continue?
cfgsync.env.confirmDiff: This restore will add {0} new file(s) and overwrite {1} changed file(s) ({2} identical).
cfgsync.env.noCaptured: No environment configs to restore (configs/ is empty). Capture them first via “Capture machine config”.
cfgsync.env.upToDate: This machine's environment configs already match the repository — nothing to restore.
cfgsync.env.subtitle: Write the SSH / GPG / git credentials / Codex / Claude environment configs captured in the repo back onto this machine (backs up before overwriting; restored private keys get their permissions tightened automatically).
cfgsync.env.title: Restore environment configs
cfgsync.remote.title: Remote deploy
cfgsync.remote.subtitle: Push this repo to another machine over SSH and run a deploy command there (uses your SSH key; no passwords).
cfgsync.ssh.register: and register the public key on GitHub
cfgsync.ssh.subtitle: Generate a local ed25519 key and apply the ssh config; the private key is never committed. The public key is registered on GitHub only when checked.
cfgsync.ssh.title: SSH (a new key is generated per device)
cfgsync.subtitle: Apply the configs from the repository to this machine (backs up automatically before overwriting). This modifies your local environment — proceed as needed.
cfgsync.title: Config Sync
